generated_oml:
// C1: Physical Sensing Components
instance HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "3D object detection sensor (UST-10LX model) integrated into upper-level chassis for LiDAR point cloud generation"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Real-time 3D perception camera (D435i model) providing depth information for sensor fusion"
    DTDFVocab:producedData DepthCameraData
]

instance ROS2StatePublishers : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "ROS2-based state publishers for ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle) and detected object tracks"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EgoVehicleStateData, ObjectTrackData
]

// C2: Physical Acting Components
instance MotorActuationFE : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Converts desired speed into motor voltage commands for ACC system implementation"
]

instance SteeringServoActuationFE : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls steering angle adjustments based on ACC logic commands"
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction Data Flows
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance DepthCameraData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance EgoVehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ROS2StatePublishers
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance ObjectTrackData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ROS2StatePublishers
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements DBSCAN for LiDAR point cloud segmentation and Kalman filtering for object track prediction"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationFE, ROS2Middleware
]

instance ACCControllingFE : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Implements ISO 15622-based control logic (Performance Class I) for speed adjustment in ACC system"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, SQLDatabase
]

instance EgoVehicleStateDataStorage : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stores real-time ego vehicle state (speed, steering angle) for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EgoVehicleStateData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

instance ObjectTrackDataStorage : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stores object tracks (position, velocity) generated by clustering and tracking FE"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ObjectTrackData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase, ExternalStorage
]

instance ProjectedDrivingPathStorage : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Stores projected driving path derived from sensor fusion for visualization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData ProjectedDrivingPathData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SQLDatabase
]

// C11: Enablers
instance DataTranslationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "ROS2-based Data Translation Feature Engine that serializes, aligns message types, and performs unit conversions (LiDAR coordinates, timestamps)"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware
]

instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Robot Operating System 2 (ROS2) middleware for distributed system orchestration and inter-component communication"
    DTDFVocab:enables DataTranslationFE, PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE, ACCControllingFE
]

instance Python38Prototyping : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Python 3.8 environment for rapid prototyping of perception tasks and data processing"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE
]

instance CPlusPlusROS2Lifecycle : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "C++ implementation for ROS2 node lifecycle management and system coordination"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, DataTranslationFE
]

instance MATLABSimulinkPerception : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MATLAB/Simulink environment for point cloud clustering and tracking implementations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE
]

instance NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Onboard compute platform (NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano) for real-time processing and AI acceleration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE, ACCControllingFE
]

instance NVIDIAJetpack5SDK : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "NVIDIA Jetpack 5.0 SDK for software development and hardware integration"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano
]

instance SQLDatabase : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SQL database for storing object tracks and ego vehicle state data for historical analysis"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackDataStorage, EgoVehicleStateDataStorage
]

instance ExternalStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Off-vehicle external storage for handling large real-time datasets"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ObjectTrackDataStorage
]

instance ISO23247ReferenceArchitecture : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Adapted ISO 23247 Reference Architecture for automotive digital twin systems"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PhysicalTwinEntityDesign
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeDataMonitoring : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle state, detected object tracks, and projected driving path via 3D Visualization FE"
    DTDFVocab:provides EgoVehicleStateVisualization, ObjectTrackVisualization, PathProjectionVisualization
]

instance ComputationOffloading : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC logic (lead vehicle detection, speed computation) from vehicle ECUs to cloud-based processing"
    DTDFVocab:provides LeadVehicleDetection, SpeedComputation
]

instance RemoteManagement : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables remote control of ACC via Control Panel UI (enable/disable, set desired speeds, emergency braking)"
    DTDFVocab:provides ACCEnableDisable, SpeedSetting, EmergencyBrakingTrigger
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance EgoVehicleStateVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time visualization of ego vehicle speed and steering angle for operator monitoring"
]

instance ObjectTrackVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Visual representation of detected objects (position, velocity) for collision avoidance assessment"
]

instance PathProjectionVisualization : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "3D visualization of projected driving path derived from sensor fusion for path planning"
]

instance ACCPerformanceAnalytics : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring of ACC performance (speed adjustments, braking events) via Reporting FE"
]

instance SpeedAdjustmentAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic speed adjustment based on lead vehicle detection and ISO 15622 compliance"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EmergencyBrakingAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic emergency braking triggered by critical distance-to-collision metrics"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance DesiredSpeedSetting : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Operator-controlled setting of desired cruise control speed via Control Panel"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ACCEnableDisableAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Remote enable/disable of adaptive cruise control functionality"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Digital Twin Entities
instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:DigitalTwinEntity [
    base:desc "Single centralized Digital Twin Entity hosting all FE components for ACC system monitoring and control"
    DTDFVocab:contains DataTranslationFE, PointCloudClusteringAndTrackingFE, ACCControllingFE, SQLDatabase
]

instance UserVisualizationEntity : DTDFVocab:UserEntity [
    base:desc "3D Visualization FE providing operator interface for real-time monitoring and reporting"
    DTDFVocab:contains EgoVehicleStateVisualization, ObjectTrackVisualization, PathProjectionVisualization
]

instance UserControlPanelEntity : DTDFVocab:UserEntity [
    base:desc "Control Panel FE enabling remote management of ACC settings and actions"
    DTDFVocab:contains DesiredSpeedSetting, ACCEnableDisableAction
]

// C1: Physical Twin Entity
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:PhysicalTwinEntity [
    base:desc "Custom Physical Twin Entity hosting domain-specific functionalities (Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE) mapping to OME concept"
    DTDFVocab:contains HokuyoUST10LXLiDAR, IntelDeepsenseD435iDepthCamera, ROS2StatePublishers, MotorActuationFE, SteeringServoActuationFE
    DTDFVocab:hosts NVIDIAJetsonOrinNano
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The digital twin interacts with the physical twin via **Wi-Fi communication** (UDP-based) to transmit control commands, including: (1) **Desired speed adjustments** from the ACC Controlling FE to the Actuation FE, (2) **Emergency braking commands** via the Control Panel UI, and (3) **Remote enable/disable signals** for ACC functionality. Actuation commands are converted into motor voltage commands by the Actuation FE and sent to the embedded controllers (e.g., speed controller) on the physical twin."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The twinning time scale operates at **real-time granularity**, with synchronization requirements: (1) **Data Collection**: Object tracks and ego vehicle state are updated every **perception cycle** (typically <100 ms), aligned with sensor refresh rates. (2) **Virtual-to-Physical Latency**: End-to-end latency from vehicle to DT Entity must be ≤100 ms (per 3GPP TS 22.185). (3) **Clock Synchronization**: Devices are synchronized to an accuracy of **≤100 ms** (due to hardware constraints), ensuring event ordering and data integrity in distributed systems."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The DT constellation consists of three primary entities from ISO 23247, with custom extensions: (1) **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts domain-specific FEs (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE, Actuation FE) for sensor/actuator integration. (2) **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data collection (Data Collecting FE), reporting (Reporting FE), and ACC control (Controlling FE). (3) **User Entity**: Split into two sub-FEs: (a) **3D Visualization FE** (User Interface FE) for real-time monitoring, (b) **Control Panel FE** (User Interface FE) for remote management. The constellation is orchestrated via ROS2 for intra-entity communication and Wi-Fi for inter-entity communication."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The DT operates primarily in the **operational phase** of the vehicle’s lifecycle, with a secondary role in the **design phase**:
  - **Design phase**: Used as a **cyber-physical experimentation facility** to validate ACC logic (ISO 15622 compliance) and simulate autonomous driving scenarios before deployment on full-scale vehicles.
  - **Operational phase**: Enables **real-time monitoring**, **remote management**, and **computation offloading** for ACC systems during vehicle operation. The system does not explicitly implement **predictive maintenance** (as mentioned in related work) but focuses on **performance optimization** (e.g., fuel economy improvements) via data-driven analytics."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity and validity are ensured through: (1) **Verification**: Real-time latency constraints (≤100 ms) and clock synchronization (≤100 ms) are verified via profiling tools. (2) **Validation**: ACC logic is validated against ISO 15622 for Performance Class I compliance. (3) **Uncertainty Handling**: Object tracks are refined via DBSCAN/Kalman filtering to mitigate sensor noise. (4) **Error Mitigation**: Data Translation FE handles unit conversions and message alignment to prevent compatibility issues. The DT’s validity is validated during the operational phase via remote monitoring and emergency braking testing."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The DT technical connections include:
  - **Onboard communication**: **ROS2 topics** (e.g., `/ego_vehicle_state`, `/object_tracks`) for intra-entity data exchange within the vehicle.
  - **Inter-entity communication**: **Wi-Fi network** (UDP-based) for DT-to-PT interactions (e.g., ACC commands).
  - **Data serialization**: **Data Translation FE** standardizes message formats (e.g., OPC UA or custom ROS2 message types) to ensure interoperability.
  - **Network protocols**: ROS2 (for onboard communication) and Wi-Fi (for remote management). No explicit mention of specific topic names, but the system ensures low-latency, synchronized data exchange."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The DT is deployed in a **hybrid architecture**: (1) **Onboard Components**: ROS2 nodes execute on the NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano for real-time processing (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking FE). (2) **Cloud Hosting**: ACC logic and compute-intensive tasks (e.g., ACC speed computation) are offloaded to cloud servers for scalability. (3) **Storage**: SQL databases store historical data externally (off-vehicle) to comply with R3 (data storage constraints). (4) **Visualization**: 3D Visualization FE runs on a local machine or cloud-based dashboard for real-time rendering."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The DT adheres to the following standards: (1) **ISO 23247**: Reference architecture for DTs (adapted for automotive), (2) **ISO 15622**: Adaptive Cruise Control performance requirements, (3) **3GPP TS 22.185**: V2V communication latency requirements (≤100 ms), (4) **ROS2**: Robot Operating System for inter-component communication. Custom extensions include the Physical Twin Entity (not defined in ISO 23247) and domain-specific FEs."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Security and safety are addressed through:
  - **Safety-critical design**: ACC logic adheres to **ISO 15622** standards, ensuring deterministic response times and emergency braking capabilities.
  - **Latency constraints**: End-to-end latency ≤100 ms to prevent delayed actuation (critical for safety).
  - **Remote management**: Operators can trigger **emergency braking** remotely, but the system ensures **deterministic response times** to avoid accidents.
  - **Clock synchronization**: ±100 ms accuracy to prevent timing-based attacks or data integrity issues.
  - **No explicit access control**: While remote operators are implied, no details on authentication/authorization are provided (not in Document)."
]
